[06:25] Typically, it's just an acquisition play, right? They just like, hey, yeah, I'm going to buy WhatsApp. I'm going to buy this. I'm going to buy that.

 

[06:32] But TikTok came in and now we have the number of MAUs that ended up happening with TikTok. Uh MAUs is monthly active users, right? For those who don't know the acronym, TikTok has one one billion, right?

[07:20] TikTok's growth. So, as I was saying, MAU's 1 billion. Basically, this is the monthly active users, but this is determined by how much time it took them to get there, right?

 

[07:31] So, for TikTok, in terms of Facebook, it took them 5.1 years to get to 1 billion monthly active users. If you look, Facebook almost took about double the time basically.

 

[07:43] So, now you see why the paradigm shift that started to happen with the other platforms trying to copy TikTok is because they're deathly scared of what what's going to happen when TikTok starts to catch up.

 

[07:55] The other thing is we have early day CPMs. What happens with early day CPMs is that TikTok right now has one-third of the users of Facebook.

[08:24] Less competition.

Like one of our clients who came to us to trademark his residential home cleaning brand in the US and Canada. You've probably seen their signs all across North America. Back then, all Dave had was three things. A dream, a vision, and a name he knew he needed to own to make his dream a reality. We did what we've done for thousands of our clients. We helped Dave trademarked his brand with a guaranteed result for a guaranteed budget. And shortly after he sold his brand to a massive billion-dollar holding company that has taken Dave's vision to a whole new level. So when you ask me, do trademarks actually matter? I can't promise you'll build a successful business just because you trademark your brand. What I can promise is that if you don't trademark your brand before somebody else does, you can kiss goodbye your dream of ever seeing your business succeed. If you want to be like Dave and protect your brand with a guaranteed result for a guaranteed budget with all office actions governed, tap the link below and talk with my team to get started. I'll see you on the other side."

[15:00] And he actually did a whole bunch of tests. [15:20] It's I was like, "Wow, that's pretty crazy. Imagine spending around like 400k to produce this and you spend like 500 and it beats out that." Not not a good spot to be that production company, I'll tell you that.

[15:57] That's why you see a lot of people say, "Oh, you know, post three to five times on TikTok." It's because you're pretty much just trying to get into the vein of going viral because once you do, magical magical things start to happen, right?

 

[16:10] Because it's a trend-based platform. What does a trend-based platform mean? When your stuff starts to, you know, go viral, the reason Russell is doing that ad is because he wants something that appeals to a whole bunch of audience, uh, eyeballs to then eventually retarget, right?

[18:50] Now, one thing that I just want to harp home, like German was saying, you could sit here and be like, "Oh, I don't want to film this TikTok. I don't want to do this. I don't want to do that." That's fine.

 

[19:00] I actually actively implore you to not fucking come on TikTok because it makes my life easier. Truly, don't do it. Like, just just keep it going. And for the ones that take action in this room, great. That's great.

 

[19:13] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Desculpas ou Dinheiro"

 

NÚMEROS: Apenas 3 criativos necessários para gerar 40 variações

 

VISUAL: Escolha binária: fazer desculpas vs fazer dinheiro, ênfase em mentalidade de ação

 

[19:16] So, you could either make excuses or you could fucking make money straight up. Like, when clients complain, I don't want to do this. I'm like, "All I need is like three creatives and I can turn this into like 40. Just film fucking three."

 

[19:30] Funny story. I think I have a lead in the presentation. I'm just going to tell it now. When When Jeremy and I were working together, he wanted he wanted six Instagram reels, right, as to run for advertising.

 

[19:42] And I I was in charge of production, so I was like, "Yo, you know, influencer that I was working with, not to be named, you probably know." He's like, "Oh, we got to film six reels." He didn't want to fucking film anything.

 

[19:54] So, I go to Jeremy. I'm like, "Jeremy, you know, XYZ didn't want to film. He didn't he didn't want to film the thing." He's like, "Get me on the phone with him right now."

 

[20:02] And then he he literally calls him. He's like, "You say you want to run a $100 million company, but you don't want to film six Instagram reels? Are you fucking kidding me? This shit generates revenue, but you don't want to film? You're not fucking serious?"

 

[20:18] Jeremy's very direct. You know him, right? It's It's crazy. So for TikTok, I see it the same way. And on TikTok, you can't even go over a minute in your ads. I'm not fucking here asking for a 43-minute webinar. I'm asking for like three minutes of fucking video that I can cut up later. That's it. That's it.

 

[20:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Segredos para Maior Vida Útil dos Anúncios no TikTok"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Lista de otimizações simples para estender vida útil dos criativos

 

[20:41] Cool. Who wants the secrets of getting a longer shelf life on TikTok? Only a few hands.

 

[20:47] Yes, sir.

 

[20:48] Okay, cool. Right there. There you go.

 

[20:51] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "1. Mudar Headline e Hook"

 

NÚMEROS: 85 centavos de cada dólar gastos em headline (David Ogilvy)

 

VISUAL: Exemplo visual de mudança de texto sobre vídeo, citação de David Ogilvy

 

[20:53] Change headline and hook. So, when you see a TikTok, typically there's text right in the beginning. Just change that out. David Ogilvy has a quote. If he had a dollar, he would spend 85 80 85 cents of it working on the headline. That's for a reason. Just change this.

 

[21:10] Do use a different angle. Use a different objection, different pain point, whatever. Change the color behind the text. Typically, just to give you context, I'm usually just doing like red text with white letters. That's like what I've seen just work the most. I've seen weird ones that are like green. They don't really convert that well.

 

[21:28] Is that a question?

 

[21:29] Yeah, go for it. When you're saying change headline and hook

 

[21:32] to be clear, are you saying take the exact same creative exact same way it is and just change the actual text?

 

[21:38] Exactly. That's exactly it. Just literally just changing the text. I'm not talking about the creative. This is all visual changes.

 

[21:45] Does it matter where you put the hashtags before the

 

[21:48] hashtags? I don't typically put hashtags on visual because I don't really you can't really like SEO them or anything. Yeah, you could put them in the captions, but it doesn't really do too too much.

 

[21:58] So, if you're going to have a client uh record you TikTok content, you want them to send that over to you without any text or any editing.

 

[22:05] Correct.

 

[22:06] Yeah. You want the raw version so that you can edit it later.

 

[22:09] Cool.

 

[22:10] Um you have let's say one video. Do you recommend coming up with let's say three to five different headlines and post that video three to five times with different headlines or more?

 

[22:21] So clarification of vernacular when you say post you mean organically or so there are ways I I'll tackle later. When you post organically, typically whenever I'm running TikTok ads, I'm always doing something called spark ads, which is you run the ad in the ads manager as if it was like an organic post, so that when somebody clicks on your profile, it goes to your your actual bio and profile, stuff like that.

 

[22:45] There are ways in TikTok that you can post an organic video and then hide it in something called ads only mode, and then it still will give you an ad authorization code that you can use inside the TikTok ads manager.

 

[22:57] still posted organic but hidden from public view. Does that make sense? It's a little confusing. They have it kind of weird.

 

[23:04] Yeah. Any other questions? Cool. Cool.

 

[23:08] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "2. Mudar CTAs Visuais"

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificado

 

VISUAL: Exemplo de CTA visual posicionado acima da dobra, evitando UI do TikTok

 

[23:10] Change visual CTAs. So, typically, and I I believe I I'm not sure if I put the ad in later, but if you're curious later, the there's usually a visual CTA. So, you didn't see it in Andre's ad because the strategy for this was a little different.

 

[23:24] Visual CTA is just And also, guys, make sure Jeremy, I don't think I could draw on this, right? Because it's a slide. No. Okay, let's just pretend there's a box here, right? Don't put stuff on the bottom because all the UI text is there, right?

 

[23:38] So, just make sure that if you do a call to action, keep it kind of like above the fold, you'll see it. Like, you don't want to compete with the text because usually if it's like white text, white on white text, even though there's a drop shadow, doesn't work out too well.

 

[23:52] So, when you're changing the visual CTAs, what you really want to think through is like instead of saying like follow me for some advertising, click on the link below, blah blah blah, because TikTok actually doesn't let you change the button text. Like on Facebook, you could choose multiple. TikTok lets you, but it it actually does a really horrible job.

 

[24:10] And what we've tested is when we put the visual CTA all throughout the TikTok, it works better. Like that's it.

 

[24:17] Is there a certain color that you see through the text?

 

[24:20] Uh it it usually depends on the video, but typically I'm just doing for visual CTAs, the funny thing is I'm typically doing red text with a white outline. Yeah. And then for the bubble text, it's usually red with white.

 

[24:34] Question. You said uh putting the call to action throughout the entire video, correct? Like having a bar with like click link for whatever.

 

[24:42] Yeah, good good clarifying question.

 

[24:44] Yeah, the caption for like the whole video.

 

[24:46] No, so it would be let's say uh like Andre, he was saying, you know, tap the link below to talk with my team. That would be displayed if we were looking at a TikTok that would just be displayed like kind of like around here or maybe on the top throughout the whole TikTok.

 

[25:02] Yeah, throughout the whole thing. You just tell them the call to action

 

[25:05] above or below like the regular like text captions that you're

 

[25:08] probably not below because it'd be kind of hard because you might compete with the UI elements

 

[25:12] above.

 

[25:13] Yeah, typically above or middle. Yeah.

 

[25:16] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "3. Mudar Duração do Vídeo"

 

NÚMEROS:

Top of funnel: 7-14 segundos

Middle of funnel: 16-32 segundos

Bottom of funnel: 7-14 segundos

 

VISUAL: Gráfico de funil mostrando durações ideais por estágio

 

[25:18] The other thing is like I was saying when you had asked getting the the raw version of the video you get that because I can change the editing length of the video. So imagine we're compounding, right? All we're compounding everything.

 

[25:30] I can change the length, change the headlines. I've seen literally ads perform where I change the length. By the way, if you're going to change the length, just go shorter. Always works. Okay.

 

[25:40] Change the length to go shorter and change out the CTAs. I've changed out the length, kept CTAs, and they performs very well.

 

[25:48] What's like the ideal length?

 

[25:50] Ah, that's really good. So, that's a bit of a nuance question. So, typically top of funnel type of ads would be shorter. So, anywhere from about 7 to 14 seconds, I would say more top of funnel. Really straight to the point like with TikTok. Don't put any fluff. Just go get straight to the point.

 

[26:08] For more middle of funnel, I would say kind of 16 to 32 seconds is kind of more of a sweet spot. And then bottom of funnel, go back to short. Go back to the original length like you know you're doing 7 to 14 seconds again. So very very snappy.

 

[26:23] You could test longer ones. I would actively encourage you to, but you should get it pretty short.

 

[26:28] Do you find the best performing ads, if you will, are like super edited or like raw almost?

 

[26:34] Yeah. So that one definitely depends. Typically, it's ones that are more edited and they're more kind of like raw in nature, like selfie, phone, less highly polished unless I've seen an ad that was highly polished work for an e-commerce brand more so and they're kind of like doing product benefits and it's like more middle of funnel than top of funnel.

 

[26:55] So, it was just more highly but it looked really good like they dropped the fruit and blah blah blah blah like nice color background things like that.

 

[27:02] Other cool tests, I mean, I don't want to recommend it too much, is like I have tested like a video horizontally and it's just like vertically. So, you have to like turn your phone. That ad, it was a beauty product actually worked very well. So, I I don't know like you could test that as one of one example. Okay, cool.

[28:38] So you're making an ad talk to gain followers. This is because there's an objective inside of TikTok to do a follower campaign. Now, you might be scarred with follower campaigns from the Facebook days, things like that. But here are my results with doing a follower campaign.

[30:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Passo 2: Seguir de Volta para DM"

 

NÚMEROS:

Limite de 15,000 pessoas seguidas antes de bugs

150-250 DMs por dia (limite da plataforma)

 

VISUAL: Processo de follow back, constraints da plataforma TikTok, estratégia de unfollowing

 

[30:38] So, one when you start to get followers, you need to follow them back because on TikTok, you have to follow them back at this point in time. You have to follow them back in order to DM them basically. So, that's kind of strategy.

 

[30:52] Just be aware there is a constraint to this to this. When you get around like 15k, like you're following 15k people, it starts to get really buggy really, really quickly.

 

[31:02] So, what you want to actively be doing with the sales team is telling them if they're disqualified and they're not getting on the phone, booking an appointment, doing whatever, unfollow them because we can follow somebody else who is going to fucking convert. So, don't don't use any tire kickers.

 

[31:18] The other thing is the other constraint that they have when you're actually when you're DMing people the DM constraint is around 150 messages a day. That's why you want to get them off the platform to continue the conversations more and more.

 

[31:32] So the constraint to this system which is kind of a good and a bad thing is you can only do around 150 to 250 DMs a day. So be very careful when the sales team this proper sales team sales team expectation management.

 

[31:47] it. Don't go and talk to the tire kickers and make sure, "Oh, are you still interested?" Talk to the people who are fucking interested because every single message matters. Unless you get them off platform, then that's cool. Then of course you close them.

So, who wants a fun hack of like how to somewhat get around compliance on TikTok? Half the room. Okay. The other half, cover your ears because clearly you weren't fucking interested.

 

[36:18] So, what I do is as soon as something gets disapproved on TikTok, I literally suit up and I get on Loom and I film a Loom with social proof of like the influencer I'm working with and saying like, "Hey, this is a legit business. This person flipped this business, is now teaching this. This is our average student success rate. This is not a fucking scam. I'm not just like doing this for whatever."

 

[36:42] Every single time I've gotten on Loom, suited up, tie everything, I've gotten my ads approved every time. It's never failed.

 

[36:50] You send it to support.

 

[36:51] Yeah, send it to just just general support. And sometimes they just auto close a ticket. I just fucking do it again. I just do it again.

[37:26] So if you're spending money with that person on other platforms, show that to TikTok. Like one of the approvals, they spent like 2.5 million in YouTube ads. I had the YouTube ads dashboard pulled up and I refreshed it on Loom.

 

[37:38] I said, "We are trying to give you this kind of money and you're rejecting me from giving your company $2.5 million." Like, do you actively know what the fuck you're doing by denying my ad? trying to literally give you this money. That's that's literally how I how I frame it.

[37:56] Yeah. And then when they deny it, I just do the same thing. I send the same loop just brute force and that that works.

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

285x ROAS alcançado com estratégia de seguidores

$500 investidos geraram $210,000+ (6 figuras)

4 centavos por seguidor nos EUA

14,000 seguidores adquiridos com $500

A cada 1,000 seguidores = 1 venda de $15,000 (Airbnb arbitrage)

1 bilhão de MAUs no TikTok (1/3 do Facebook)

TikTok atingiu 1 bilhão de usuários em 5.1 anos (metade do tempo do Facebook)

1/6 dos anunciantes comparado ao Facebook (6x menos competição)

Facebook aumentou vídeo nos feeds em 50-70%

150-250 DMs por dia (limite da plataforma)

15,000 seguidos antes de bugs no sistema

3 pin posts disponíveis no perfil

9x ROAS em B2B (Trademark Factory)

$400k produção profissional vs $50 produção caseira (pancake flip venceu)

2 setters + 1 closer (estrutura de time recomendada)

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

 

Ad Talk Strategy (Estratégia Híbrida)

Criar TikToks que funcionam como anúncios pagos

Usar objetivo de "follower campaign"

Segmentação broad nos EUA

Usar Spark Ads para manter perfil orgânico

 

Processo de 3 Passos:

Passo 1: Fazer Ad Talk para ganhar seguidores

Passo 2: Seguir de volta para desbloquear DMs

Passo 3: DM → Qualificar → Fechar venda

 

Otimizações para Vida Útil do Criativo:

Mudar headline e hook (David Ogilvy: 85 centavos de cada dólar em headline)

Mudar CTAs visuais (red text + white outline funciona melhor)

Mudar duração do vídeo (sempre encurtar)

Testar diferentes pin posts

Testar diferentes bio link pages

 

Durações Ideais por Estágio:

Top of funnel: 7-14 segundos (direto ao ponto, sem fluff)

Middle of funnel: 16-32 segundos (sweet spot)

Bottom of funnel: 7-14 segundos (voltar para curto)

 

DM Magic Strategy:

CTA visual: "DM me [palavra-chave]" (ex: "DM me PROFIT")

Usar show up bribe/lead magnet

Capturar número de telefone para sair da plataforma

Unfollow não-qualificados para liberar espaço

Priorizar hand-raisers (quem levanta a mão primeiro)

 

Hack de Compliance:

Vestir terno e gravata

Gravar Loom video

Mostrar prova social (licença, testemunhos, spend em outras plataformas)

Abrir múltiplas tabs com evidências

Argumentar que está tentando dar dinheiro ao TikTok

Reenviar se negarem (brute force)

 

ESTRUTURA:

 

Constraints da Plataforma:

Limite de 15k seguindo antes de bugs

150-250 DMs por dia máximo

Sem API para automação de mensagens

Precisa follow back para DM

Ads Only Mode para esconder posts orgânicos

 

Estrutura de Time:

2 setters

1 closer

Time precisa gerenciar unfollows ativamente

Foco em qualificados, não tire-kickers

 

Tipos de Criativo:

Raw/selfie style > altamente polido (na maioria dos casos)

E-commerce pode usar mais polido para middle funnel

Evitar fluff, ir direto ao ponto

Apenas 3 criativos necessários para gerar 40 variações

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

 

Caso Trademark Factory (Andre):

9x ROAS em B2B

Nicho "boring" (marcas registradas)

Closers ficaram ANIMADOS com leads do TikTok (raro)

 

Caso Airbnb Arbitrage:

$500 investimento inicial

14,000 seguidores a $0.04 cada

Conversão: 1 venda de $15k por 1,000 seguidores

Resultado: $210,000+ (6 figuras de lucro)

ROI: 420x+ (42,000%)

 

Comparativo de Plataformas:

CPMs iniciais mais baixos que Facebook

6x menos competição

Mesma quantidade de eyeballs (considerando unduplicated reach)

 

Oportunidade Futura:

TikTok Fulfillment (similar a Amazon FBA)

1-2 anos até implementação completa

Potencial de estar early no próximo FBA boom

 

DURAÇÃO: ~43 minutos FORMATO: Apresentação ao vivo em evento com Q&A interativo
